task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay, and I want to make sure it covers the key point without creating extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft a simple message that gets the update across clearly?

prefs.explanation: He values practicality and clear communication, so he likely wants answers that get to the point without extra filler. His work habits suggest he appreciates efficiency over extended discussion. He may still accept some detail when it helps avoid mistakes, but not long-winded explanations.

He keeps glossaries, checklists, and paperwork, which suggests he responds well to structured output. A clean format would fit his preference for reliability and easy reference. He probably dislikes rambling prose when a simple list or step-by-step layout would do.

His style appears grounded in clarity and avoiding ambiguity, especially when wording matters. He likely wants the assistant to say what it means plainly rather than hedge excessively. Overly soft or evasive phrasing would probably feel unhelpful to him.

His life attitude is quiet and cautious rather than emotionally expressive, so a steady tone fits best. He likely responds well to calm professionalism more than enthusiasm or hype. He may appreciate warmth, but only in a restrained, respectful way.

prefs.short: Wants enough detail to act, not exhaustive digressions
prefs.explanation: He seems to prefer information that is usable and concrete, especially when deadlines or paperwork are involved. Too much background would likely slow him down, but too little detail risks errors. He probably wants the main point plus any important caveats, not a full lecture.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple version:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is running behind schedule because of [brief reason]. I expect to send it by [new date/time].

I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it a little firmer or more polished.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and practical: "here’s a simple version" and a short template with no filler.
Well structured as a clean message template with clear line breaks and a simple format, though not a checklist.
Straightforward and plainly stated: "the deliverable is running behind schedule" and "I expect to send it by [new date/time]."
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout; no hype or emotional language.
Gives enough detail to act: reason for delay, new date/time, and a brief follow-up line, without digressing.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version you can send as-is:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is delayed due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it to you by [new date/time].

I’ll let you know if that changes.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even firmer or more specific to the situation.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and practical: "here’s a tighter version you can send as-is" and the draft itself is brief.
Uses a clear, structured template with line breaks and placeholders, though it’s not a checklist per se.
Straightforward and explicit: "the deliverable is delayed due to [brief reason]" and "I’m working to get it to you by [new date/time]."
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout; no hype or emotional language.
Provides enough detail to act—reason, new date/time, and a follow-up promise—without extra digression.

turns.agent_response:
Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is delayed and will be sent by [new date/time].

Best,  
[Your Name]

per_pref.reason: Very concise and no-fluff: "Quick update: the deliverable is delayed and will be sent by [new date/time]."
Clean, simple structure with greeting, update, and sign-off; not a checklist, but organized and easy to use.
Straightforward and explicit: "the deliverable is delayed" and "will be sent by [new date/time]" with no hedging.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout; neutral wording without hype or emotional language.
Provides enough detail to act by stating the delay and the new date/time, while avoiding unnecessary background.
